channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Faible] Vietnam arrests suspects behind HiAnime anime piracy service
item.description: ​Vietnamese authorities have arrested and are prosecuting seven suspects believed to have run HiAnime, the largest anime piracy streaming service before its shutdown in June. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Fake IT support calls on Microsoft Teams push EtherRAT malware
item.description: Threat actors are abusing Microsoft Teams voice calls by impersonating corporate IT support staff to trick employees into installing the EtherRAT malware, giving attackers initial access to corporate networks. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Phishing poses as big-brand job interview to steal Google accounts
item.description: A phishing campaign is impersonating more than 30 well-known brands, including Adobe, Netflix, Coca-Cola, and OpenAI, in fake job interviews to steal Google account credentials from marketing professionals. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Threat Actors Probe Gitea Docker Flaw CVE-2026-20896 13 Days After Disclosure
item.description:
Threat actors have been observed attempting to exploit a recently patched critical security flaw in Gitea Docker images, according to Sysdig.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-20896 (CVSS score: 9.8), a…

item.title: [Faible] 16-Year-Old Linux KVM Flaw Lets Guest VMs Escape to Host on Intel and AMD x86 Systems
item.description:
A use-after-free bug in Linux's KVM hypervisor can be triggered from a guest virtual machine to corrupt the shadow-page state of the host kernel that runs it.
Dubbed 'Januscape' and tracked as CVE-2026-53359, the…

item.title: [Faible] Iran-Linked Hackers Use New Cavern C2 Framework to Target Israeli Organizations
item.description: An Iranian hacking group affiliated with Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS) has been wielding a previously undocumented modular command-and-control (C2) framework dubbed Cavern (aka Cav3rn) targeting…

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-28318 — SolarWinds Serv-U Uncontrolled Resource Consumption Vulnerability
item.description: SolarWinds Serv-U contains an uncontrolled resource consumption vulnerability that allows specially crafted POST requests using the Content-Encoding: deflate header to crash the Serv-U service without authentication.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-50751 — Check Point Security Gateway Improper Authentication Vulnerability
item.description: Check Point Security Gateway contains an improper authentication vulnerability in IKEv1 key exchange that could allow an unauthenticated remote attacker to bypass user authentication and establish a remote access VPN connection without a valid user password.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-42271 — BerriAI LiteLLM Command Injection Vulnerability
item.description: BerriAI LiteLLM contains a command injection vulnerability that could allow any authenticated user, including holders of low-privilege internal-user keys, to run arbitrary commands on the host.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-20245 — Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output Vulnerability
item.description: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager formerly SD-WAN vManage contains an improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability. This vulnerability could allow an authenticated, local attacker to execute arbitrary commands as root by supplying a crafted file to the affected system.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-7473 — Arista Extensible Operating System Incomplete Comparison with Missing Factors Vulnerability
item.description: Arista Extensible Operating System (EOS) contains an incomplete comparison with missing factors vulnerability when the switch incorrectly decapsulate and forwards other unexpected tunneled packet with a destination IP matching its configured decapsulation IP.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-11645 — Google Chromium V8 Out-of-Bounds Read and Write Vulnerability
item.description: Google Chromium V8 out-of-bounds read and write vulnerability that could allow a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code inside a sandbox via a crafted HTML page. This vulnerability could affect multiple web browsers that utilize Chromium, including, but not limited to, Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, and Opera.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-10520 — Ivanti Sentry OS Command Injection Vulnerability
item.description: Ivanti Sentry (formerly known as MobileIron Sentry) contains an OS command injection vulnerability which could allow a remote unauthenticated user to achieve root-level remote code execution. This vulnerability can be successfully exploited in cases where the Sentry appliance is in an unmanaged state with its endpoints externally reachable. The use of mTLS with EPMM or restricted HTTPS access through Neurons for MDM makes interfaces inaccessible to external actors.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-35273 — Oracle PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools Missing Authentication for Critical Function Vulnerability
item.description: Oracle PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools contains a missing authentication for critical function vulnerability which could allow an unauthenticated attacker to obtain takeover of PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-20262 — Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager Directory or Path Traversal Vulnerability
item.description: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager contains a directory or path traversal vulnerability that could allow an authenticated, remote attacker to create a file or overwrite any file on the filesystem of an affected system.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-54420 — LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin UNIX Symbolic Link (Symlink) Following Vulnerability
item.description: LiteSpeed cPanel plugin contains a UNIX symbolic link (Symlink) following vulnerability that could allow a user with FTP or web shell access on a shared hosting server running CloudLinux/CageFS.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-48907 — Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor Improper Access Control Vulnerability
item.description: Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor contains an improper access control vulnerability which could allow for upload and execution of PHP code via the creation of new editor profiles for unauthenticated users.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-20253 — Splunk Enterprise Missing Authentication for Critical Function Vulnerability
item.description: Splunk Enterprise contains a missing authentication for critical function vulnerability which could allow an unauthenticated user to create or truncate arbitrary files through a PostgreSQL sidecar service endpoint.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-34908 — Ubiquiti UniFi OS Improper Access Control Vulnerability
item.description: Ubiquiti UniFi OS contains an improper access control vulnerability which could allow a malicious actor with access to the network to make unauthorized changes to the system.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-34909 — Ubiquiti UniFi OS Path Traversal Vulnerability
item.description: Ubiquiti UniFi OS contains a path traversal vulnerability which could allow a malicious actor with access to the network to access files on the underlying system that could be manipulated to access an underlying account.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-34910 — Ubiquiti UniFi OS Improper Input Validation Vulnerability
item.description: Ubiquiti UniFi OS contains an improper input validation vulnerability which could allow a malicious actor with access to the network to conduct command injection.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2025-67038 — Lantronix EDS5000 Code Injection Vulnerability
item.description: Lantronix EDS5000 contains a code injection vulnerability that could allow attackers to inject arbitrary OS commands into the username parameter. Injected commands are executed with root privileges.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-20230 — Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) Vulnerability
item.description: Cisco Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) and Cisco Unified Communications Manager Session Management Edition (Unified CM SME) contain a server-side request forgery (SSRF) Vulnerability that could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to write files to the underlying operating system that could be used later to elevate to root.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-12569 — PTC Windchill and FlexPLM Improper Input Validation Vulnerability
item.description: PTC Windchill and FlexPLM contains an improper input validation vulnerability allowing an unauthenticated, remote attacker to execute arbitrary code by sending a malicious request to the network.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-48558 — SimpleHelp Authentication Bypass Vulnerability
item.description: SimpleHelp contains an authentication bypass vulnerability in the OIDC authentication flow. When OIDC authentication is configured, identity tokens submitted during login are accepted without verifying their cryptographic signature. In a vulnerable configuration, a remote, unauthenticated attacker can submit a forged token containing arbitrary identity claims to obtain a fully authenticated technician session. In some configurations, this may also allow bypass of multi-factor authentication.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-45659 — Microsoft SharePoint Server Deserialization of Untrusted Data Vulnerability
item.description: Microsoft SharePoint Server contains a deserialization of untrusted data vulnerability which allows an authorized attacker to execute code over a network.

item.title: [Faible] Polymarket face au soupçon d’initiés
item.description: Polymarket visé par une affaire d’initié Google et une interdiction en Espagne : enjeux cyber et renseignement.

item.title: [Faible] FIOD : deux arrestations dans une affaire de sanctions
item.description: Deux suspects arrêtés pour soupçon de contournement des sanctions européennes contre la russie. 800 serveurs saisis.

item.title: [Faible] Fuite présumée sur la Carte Jeune Occitanie
item.description: Fuite présumée de 318 751 données liées à la Carte Jeune Occitanie : risques, prudence et enjeu cyber pour les élèves et les parents.

item.title: [Faible] Tripadvisor, Bet365 et Zalando : trois fuites revendiquées et beaucoup d’ombre
item.description: Fuites revendiquées chez Tripadvisor, Bet365 et Zalando Australia : attribution faible, prudence cyber maximale.

item.title: [Faible] Fuite présumée chez bet365 : l’ombre d’un fichier trés sensible ?
item.description: Un pirate affirme vendre 120 millions de profils attribués à bet365. La nature des données évoquée par le hacker pourrait sembler bien plus grave qu’une "simple" liste d’adresses électroniques.

item.title: [Faible] Akaolife, l’intrusion qui menace France Travail ?
item.description: Intrusion revendiquée chez Akaolife : données RH, santé et France Travail au cœur d’un risque cyber majeur ?

item.title: [Faible] LeHACK 2026, la scène hacker éthique française en alerte
item.description: LeHACK 2026 réunit hackers et experts cyber, avec le confessionnal ZATAZ pour signaler failles et fuites.

item.title: [Critique] L’authentification par certificat pour Active Directory : pourquoi elle devient incontournable avec la dépréciation de NTLM
item.description: Microsoft met fin à NT LAN Manager (NTLM), mais le remplacer dans les systèmes legacy, les applications et les intégrations tierces n’est pas une opération simple. Pour les accès distants exposés sur internet en…

item.title: [Faible] Cyber actualités ZATAZ de la semaine du 28 juin 2026
item.description: Bonjour à toutes et tous,  Cette semaine cyber est marquée par de nouvelles fuites revendiquées, des intrusions sensibles et des opérations judiciaires d’ampleur. Akaolife, Bet365, Tripadvisor, Zalando Australia,…

item.title: [Faible] Faux résultats du bac : l’IA durcit l’arnaque
item.description: Faux sites du bac : données personnelles, IA et publicité exploitent l’impatience des candidats avant les résultats.

item.title: [Élevé] CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts…

item.title: [Élevé] Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
item.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of…

item.title: [Élevé] Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published…

item.title: [Faible] Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation…

item.title: [Critique] Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on…

item.title: [Moyen] A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
item.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly…

item.title: [Critique] Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises…

item.title: [Critique] ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
item.description: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts.…

item.title: [Faible] Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
item.description: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the…

item.title: [Élevé] FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet
item.description: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) said today it worked with industry partners to seize hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a sprawling residential proxy service operated by the publicly-traded Israeli…

item.title: [Faible] Alleged Scattered Spider hacker extradited to the United States
item.description: A dual United States and Estonian citizen has been extradited to the U.S. to face charges alleging he was a member of the Scattered Spider hacking collective. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Opera rolls out Paste Protect feature to fight ClickFix attacks
item.description: Opera has introduced Paste Protect, a security feature designed to block ClickFix-style attacks that trick users into executing malicious commands through social engineering. [...]

item.title: [Critique] CISA: Microsoft SharePoint RCE flaw now actively exploited
item.description: CISA warned on Wednesday that attackers have begun exploiting a high-severity Microsoft SharePoint remote code execution vulnerability patched in May. [...]

item.title: [Moyen] Cisco finally confirms attackers exploiting Unified CM flaw
item.description: Cisco confirmed that attackers are now exploiting a Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) vulnerability patched in early June. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft fixes bug that removed Copilot buttons in Outlook
item.description: Microsoft has fixed a known issue causing the Copilot Chat or Copilot buttons in Classic Outlook to disappear for Windows users with the Copilot Chat (Basic) license. [...]

item.title: [Faible] ConsentFix and ClickFix: How Microsoft 365 Accounts are Hijacked in 3 Seconds
item.description: ConsentFix and ClickFix attacks steal Microsoft 365 tokens in seconds using fake prompts and OAuth flows. Learn how these MFA bypass tactics work and how to defend against them. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Google loses final appeal to overturn €4.1 billion EU fine
item.description: Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) has dismissed Google's final appeal against a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) antitrust fine over the company's use of Android to promote its Chrome browser and search service.…

item.title: [Faible] Claude Fable relaunch disappoints users with nerfed performance
item.description: Claude Fable, the company's most powerful model, is now available to all users, but early impressions are disappointing, as it appears to be nowhere near the original release. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Claude Fable 5 isn’t permanently leaving subscriptions, Anthropic says
item.description: Anthropic says Claude Fable 5 won't be accessible via Claude subscriptions after July 7, but it's not a permanent change, and the company expects the model to return outside the usage-based plan soon. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] ARToken PhaaS exposes EvilTokens' Microsoft 365 phishing toolkit
item.description: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform dubbed "ARToken" appears to operate as an affiliate of the EvilTokens phishing platform, giving researchers a glimpse into an extensive toolkit designed to…

item.title: [Élevé] NetNut proxy network disrupted, 2 million infected devices cut off
item.description: A joint operation involving Google has disrupted NetNut, a residential proxy network that gave access to millions of compromised Android devices, including smart TVs and streaming boxes. [...]

item.title: [Critique] JadePuffer ransomware used AI agent to automate entire attack
item.description: Researchers identified what they believe is the first documented case of a ransomware operation, JadePuffer, conducted entirely by a large language model (LLM) agent. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Flipper Zero firmware development continues with community help
item.description: Flipper Devices says development of the Flipper Zero firmware will continue, albeit with a smaller internal team and greater reliance on community contributions. [...]

item.title: [Moyen] Max severity Adobe ColdFusion flaw now exploited in attacks
item.description: Attackers are now exploiting a maximum-severity Adobe ColdFusion vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-48282, according to vulnerability intelligence company KEVIntel. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Software Is Now Written at the Speed of Thought. Security Isn't.
item.description: Every evolution in software development has reduced the friction between an idea and a deployable application. AI may remove the final barrier, but it also removes many of the moments where security decisions have…

item.title: [Faible] Ousaban Banking Trojan Targets Iberian Bank Users with Fake PDF Lures
item.description:
A Brazilian banking trojan called Ousaban is going after Windows users who bank in Spain and Portugal. Fortinet's FortiGuard Labs identified the campaign in May 2026.
It opens with a phishing PDF disguised as…

item.title: [Élevé] VEIL#DROP Malware Chain Uses Blogger Platform to Deliver PureLogs Stealer
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new multi-stage malware delivery attack chain that uses social engineering and Blogger pages to deliver an information stealer called PureLogs.
The activity has been codenamed…

item.title: [Faible] SEO-Poisoned Software Sites Abuse ScreenConnect to Deploy AsyncRAT
item.description:
Unknown threat actors are leveraging the ScreenConnect remote access tool as a way to deploy and execute AsyncRAT.
Kaspersky said the activity is part of a "massive, multi-domain, multi-language" campaign that…

item.title: [Faible] 19-Year-Old Scattered Spider Suspect Extradited to Face U.S. Hacking Charges
item.description: A teenager accused of belonging to the hacking group Scattered Spider has been extradited from Finland to face U.S. charges of conspiracy, computer intrusion, and fraud, the U.S. Department of…

item.title: [Moyen] Unpatched Argo CD Repo-Server Flaw Could Let Attackers Take Over Kubernetes Clusters
item.description: Argo CD, a widely used tool for deploying software to Kubernetes, has an unpatched flaw in its repo-server component that lets an unauthenticated attacker run code, provided they can reach the component's internal…

item.title: [Critique] SharePoint RCE CVE-2026-45659 Added to CISA KEV After Active Exploitation
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a high-severity flaw impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of…

item.title: [Moyen] New ChocoPoC RAT Targets Vulnerability Researchers via Fake PoC Exploit Repos
item.description: Attackers are hiding a data-stealing trojan inside fake exploit code aimed at the people who hunt bugs for a living. The malware, called ChocoPoC, travels in Python proof-of-concept (PoC) repositories on GitHub that…

item.title: [Critique] FortiBleed Credential Theft Linked to INC and Lynx Ransomware Operations
item.description:
The recently discovered financially-motivated FortiBleed campaign has been attributed to INC and Lynx ransomware operations, indicating that the verified, stolen credentials were intended for follow-on intrusions.
"An…

item.title: [Critique] AI Agent Exploits Langflow RCE to Automate Database Ransomware Attack
item.description:
Security firm Sysdig says it has found what it believes is the first ransomware attack run from start to finish by an AI agent.
Its Threat Research Team calls the operator JADEPUFFER and says a large language…

item.title: [Faible] Identity Lifecycle Management Wasn't Built for AI Agents
item.description: Identity lifecycle management was architected around a person with an employment record, a manager, and a departure date. AI agents have none of those. As autonomous principals proliferate across enterprise…

item.title: [Élevé] ToddyCat-Linked Umbrij Malware Abuses OAuth to Access Gmail via Google API
item.description:
The threat actor known as ToddyCat has been attributed to a new malware called Umbrij that's designed to gain surreptitious access to a victim's email correspondence via the Google API.
"In this campaign, the attackers…

item.title: [Critique] ThreatsDay: AI Compute Hijacking, Apple Email Flaw, BlueHammer Ransomware + 14 Stories
item.description:
This week’s security news is mostly about weak spots.
Browsers, bots, sandboxes, AI systems, and email flows all show the same problem in different ways. Everything looks normal until someone tests a small gap and…

item.title: [Critique] Ransomware Groups Turn to Citrix Bleed 2, BYOVD, and Supply Chain Credentials
item.description:
Threat actors associated with the Anubis ransomware operation have been observed exploiting the Citrix Bleed 2 (CVE-2025-5777) vulnerability to obtain initial access.
"Although tactics differ between affiliates, common…

item.title: [Faible] Google Disrupts NetNut Residential Proxy Network Spanning 2 Million Home Devices
item.description:
Google has significantly degraded NetNut, one of the biggest networks that turns home devices into rented relays for other people's traffic.
Working with the FBI, Lumen, and others, Google's Threat Intelligence Group…

item.title: [Critique] PamStealer Uses Fake Maccy Sites and PAM Checks to Steal Mac Login Passwords
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called PamStealer that employs a series of clever tricks to infect systems and siphon sensitive data.
The stealer, discovered by Jamf Threat Labs,…

item.title: [Faible] European Parliament Member Investigating Spyware Was Hacked With Pegasus
item.description: A new report from the Citizen Lab has revealed that former Member of the European Parliament Stelios Kouloglou had his mobile device repeatedly hacked with the notorious Pegasus spyware while serving on a committee that…

item.title: [Faible] Armored Likho Targets Government Agencies, Power Sector with BusySnake Stealer
item.description:
A previously undocumented threat actor known as Armored Likho has been attributed to cyber attacks targeting government agencies and the electric power sector across Russia, Brazil, and Kazakhstan.
"Armored Likho…

item.title: [Faible] North Korea-Linked npm Packages Mimic Rollup Polyfills to Steal Developer Secrets
item.description:
Threat actors with ties to North Korea have been linked to a fresh set of malicious npm packages that masquerade as Rollup polyfill tooling to facilitate remote access and data theft.
According to JFrog, the packages…

item.title: [Critique] New Avalon Malware Framework Packs CrownX Ransomware Capabilities
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented modular malware framework codenamed Avalon that's distributed by means of a multi-stage phishing chain capable of bypassing traditional security…

item.title: [Faible] New "Bad Epoll" Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Unprivileged Users Gain Root, Hits Android
item.description: A newly disclosed Linux kernel flaw called Bad Epoll (CVE-2026-46242) lets an ordinary user with no special access take full control of a machine as root. It affects Linux desktops, servers, and Android, and a fix is…

item.title: [Moyen] Unpatched Flaws Disclosed in Filesystem Bundled Into Millions of Embedded Devices
item.description:
Security firm runZero has disclosed seven vulnerabilities in FatFs, a small filesystem library that lets a device read and write the FAT and exFAT formats used on USB drives and SD cards.
The flaws…

item.title: [Élevé] North Korean Hackers Publish 108 Malicious Packages and Extensions in PolinRider Campaign
item.description: The North Korean threat actors linked to the Contagious Interview campaign have been observed publishing 108 unique packages and web browser extensions spanning npm, Packagist, Go, and Google Chrome as part of an…

item.title: [Critique] U.S. Government Entity Paid Kairos $1 Million in Data-Theft Extortion Case
item.description: A U.S. government entity paid about $1 million to keep stolen files from being leaked, according to a new case study by Rakesh Krishnan for Ransom-ISAC, built on a leaked negotiation chat and the blockchain trail…

item.title: [Faible] SkillCloak Lets Malicious AI Agent Skills Evade Static Scanners with Self-Extracting Packing
item.description: Scanners meant to catch malicious add-on "skills" for AI coding agents can be fooled by a few simple changes that leave the malware working, according to a new study from researchers at the Hong Kong…

item.title: [Moyen] Opera GX Flaw Let Malicious Sites Auto-Install Mods to Steal Data From Visited Pages
item.description: Researchers found a flaw in Opera GX, the gaming-focused version of the Opera browser, that let a malicious website silently install a browser add-on and use it to lift specific data from the pages a victim…

item.title: [Faible] New Java-Based QuimaRAT MaaS Built to Run on Windows, Linux, and macOS
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a novel Java-based remote access trojan (RAT) called QuimaRAT that's capable of targeting Windows, Linux, and macOS environments.
According to LevelBlue, the cross-platform…

item.title: [Faible] New TrojPix Attack Leaks Data From Air-Gapped Systems via Video Cable Emissions
item.description: Researchers at Shandong University have shown a fast new way to pull data off computers that are cut off from every network. The technique, called TrojPix, tweaks on-screen pixels in ways the eye cannot…

item.title: [Élevé] Suspected China-Nexus Hackers Use Fake Indian Tax Filing Utility to Deploy DcRAT
item.description: A suspected China-nexus threat activity cluster has been observed targeting Indian taxpayers, tax professionals, and corporate finance teams to deliver a remote access trojan designed to steal sensitive data from…

item.title: [Faible] How to Evaluate an AI SOC Platform in 2026: 6 Capabilities That Separate Leaders from Bolt-On AI solutions
item.description: Building a shortlist for an AI SOC evaluation can be tough. SIEM, SOAR, and pureplay AI SOC vendors are all saying the same thing. But behind the identical label sit very different products, from chat assistants bolted…

item.title: [Critique] ⚡ Weekly Recap: Proxy Botnets, Browser Ransomware, AI Agent Tricks, Fake PoC Malware and More
item.description:
A streaming box should not need a threat model. Neither should a username field, a demo repo, a reset flow, or a browser permission prompt. That is the irritating part this week: the risky pieces were ordinary.
Home…
